module.name: build
module.spec:
Overview: BuildListResult constructs a ListResult by resolving file content for each spec and marker when verbose mode is requested. Presenters consume the fully-resolved result; they never perform I/O themselves.
Defined terms: verbose — when true, content previews are read from disk and stored in SpecContents / MarkerContents maps; when false, both maps are nil
Requirements: R1. BuildListResult MUST skip specs and markers marked Deleted (no content read attempted for missing files). R2. When verbose is false, BuildListResult MUST return a ListResult with nil SpecContents and nil MarkerContents. The presenter renders the compact view. R3. When verbose is true, BuildListResult MUST populate SpecContents[specID] and MarkerContents[markerID] for every non-deleted spec and marker. Entries whose content read fails MUST be silently omitted (partial result is preferred over hard failure for list output). R4. BuildListResult MUST NOT return an error; content-read failures are best-effort.
See also: cli.list_format.

Overview: BuildShowResult constructs a ShowResult for a single spec or marker identified by ID, resolving content and linked entities from state and disk. Used by `drift show <id>`.
Defined terms: isSpec — heuristic: ID contains exactly one dot → spec; no dot → marker
Requirements: R1. BuildShowResult MUST dispatch on isSpecID(id): spec IDs (containing a dot) route to buildShowSpecResult; marker IDs (no dot) route to buildShowMarkerResult. R2. If the primary entity is not found in state, BuildShowResult MUST return a ShowResult with nil Spec and nil Marker and a nil error. The caller (drift show command) sets exit code 1. R3. If the primary entity's content cannot be read from disk, BuildShowResult MUST return a non-nil error wrapping the read failure. The caller maps this to exit code 1. R4. For a spec result, BuildShowResult MUST resolve all linked markers (markers whose From points to this spec) and their content. Linked-marker content reads that fail MUST be silently omitted (R3 applies only to the primary entity). R5. For a marker result, BuildShowResult MUST resolve all linked specs (specs whose ID appears as To in any edge with this marker as From) and their content. Linked-spec content reads that fail MUST be silently omitted. R6. Inbound and outbound spec-spec references (OutboundRefs, InboundRefs) MUST be computed from the baseline edge set, deduplicated, and order-stable (insertion order from the edge slice).
See also: cli.show_command.

Overview: EntityExists and DiffEdgeExists query state for the existence of nodes and edges, used by command dispatch to set exit codes without constructing full results.
Requirements: R1. EntityExists(state, id) MUST return true if and only if id matches a spec ID (when id contains a dot) or a marker ID (when id has no dot) in state. R2. DiffEdgeExists(state, markerID, specID) MUST return true if and only if both the named marker and the named spec exist in state. R3. sortEdgesByFromTo MUST sort edges lexicographically by (From, To) using insertion sort. The sort MUST be stable to preserve deterministic output across state-store iteration orders.
See also: cli.show_command, cli.list_format.
